nodes.name: Devi
forms: Goddess of the Land of Liberation

beats.title: The Awakener — Front Matter
beats.summary: This chapter is the front matter of 'The Awakener', an authorized biography of Shailendra Sharma, the fifth Guru of Kriya Yoga. It introduces the lineage of Kriya Yoga, the need for a true Guru, and the challenges of spiritual seeking in the modern world. It includes a dedication, a quote from the Bhagavad Gita, and a narrative of the author's first encounter with Shailendra Sharma.

beats.title: The Awakener — Front Matter (part 2)
beats.summary: The chapter describes the author's first encounter with his Guru, Shailendra Sharma, at an ashram in India. It details the transformative experience of meeting the Teacher, the author's journey to the ashram, and the initial impressions of the Guru's personality and teachings. The narrative also includes background on the Guru's early life and the author's reflections on writing a biography.

beats.title: The Awakener — Front Matter (part 3)
beats.summary: This chapter is a biographical narrative about the early life of Shailendra Sharma, focusing on his family background, childhood experiences, and formative events. It describes his father's career as a judge, his mother's strength, the family's noble lineage as descendants of Ashvatthama, and their non-ritualistic but scripturally knowledgeable Brahmin-Kshatriya heritage. Key episodes include his grandfather's death with a vision of luminous spheres, a friendship with a laborer's son that taught him about social hierarchy, and his early school days.

beats.title: The Awakener — Front Matter (part 4)
beats.summary: This chapter is a biographical narrative about the early life and family background of the author (presumably a yogi or spiritual teacher). It describes his childhood in a large joint family in Gwalior, the story of a devoted servant couple, the legend of the Gwalior fort and jinn, his passion for reading, his lack of interest in formal education, and the history of his great-grandfather Tahat Singh who lost his jagir after killing British soldiers. It also recounts the spiritual journey of his grandfather Gulab Singh, who sought a guru and eventually returned to worldly life.

beats.title: The Awakener — The Dying Man, the Wounded Bull, and the Absolution of Monkey Sins
beats.summary: The chapter recounts several incidents at a pilgrimage site where the narrator and Devahuti practice compassion: saving an unconscious stranger, nursing a wounded bull, and witnessing monkeys bringing their dead. The climax is a dying monkey performing an ancient ritual of surrender with dry grass in its mouth, seeking forgiveness. The narrator grants forgiveness, and the monkey dies peacefully after receiving final rites.

beats.title: The Awakener — Gandhiji
beats.summary: This chapter describes the life and death of a bull named Gandhi, who lived at a smashan (cremation ground) ashram. It covers his arrival as a calf, his behavior, his role in the ashram, his death from a tumor, and the aftermath. The chapter also recounts an ISKCON festival held at the ashram and the matchmaking attempts directed at the ashram's master.

beats.title: The Awakener — The Magic Baroncha
beats.summary: The chapter describes the arrival of abandoned cows at the smashan, their care, and the mystical properties of baronchas (hairballs) that cows give when happy. It also reflects on the spiritual significance of cow service and the rare ability to choose one's next incarnation.

beats.title: The Keepers of the Archives
beats.summary: The chapter describes the genealogy of a Brahmin family descended from Ashvatthama, as recorded by a special clan of archivists. The family's roots trace back to ancient Tibet and the inner circle of Lord Rama. The archivists confirm that Shiva is the family deity and that their worship has continued unbroken. The narrative culminates with the impending arrival of the Lord of the Universe and the family's Ishta Devata.

beats.title: The Awakener — Vultures in the Shade of the Tree
beats.summary: The chapter describes the aftermath of a flood in 1996 that devastated the smashan (cremation ground) and its memorial palaces. Devahuti, a resident, cares for dying animals and performs rituals. The Shiva temple is restored with a new Shivalingam in 1999. The narrative reflects on death, decay, and the cycle of life.

beats.title: The Awakener — Games of the Gods
beats.summary: The narrator recounts a terrifying but real encounter with Vishnu and Shiva in 1996, where Vishnu appears upon summons, then Shiva intervenes angrily, snatching him from Vishnu. The experience leaves physical marks and initiates inner and outer changes, including a flood that transforms the land and the eventual discovery of a Shivalingam.

beats.title: The Awakener — The Coming of Yamuna
beats.summary: The chapter describes a severe water crisis at the smashan, leading the narrator to invoke the river Yamuna through an offering. A flood ensues, endangering the cows and the narrator, who is struck by a python. Hanuman appears, gathers dark spirits, and heals the narrator. The flood ultimately improves the land's fertility.

beats.title: The Awakener — Puja to Hanuman
beats.summary: The chapter describes a series of events from 1992 to 2008 involving a guru (Yogiraj) and his interactions with Hanuman through mediums and rituals. It covers the initiation of an astrologer's son, the transfer of mediumistic abilities, direct communication with Hanuman, a 12-year annual puja at a smashan pond in Govardhan, and the deepening of the guru's understanding of karma and the spirit world.

beats.title: The Awakener — Prayaschitta. Abhishek. Milk
beats.summary: The chapter describes a purification ritual (prayaschitta) performed at a pond in a smashan (cremation ground) before a puja to Hanuman. The initiates bathe, have their heads shaved, and are smeared with ritual substances. The water turns milky-white due to milk offerings, which is linked to a local legend about Manasi Ganga. The chapter draws an analogy between extracting ghee from milk and the yogic process of realizing the immortal soul through Kriya-Yoga.

beats.title: The Awakener — The Second Meeting with Babaji
beats.summary: The narrator recounts his second meeting with the immortal yogi Babaji on a cold December night in 1995. Babaji appears unexpectedly, accompanied by a silent companion, and gives him a Shri Yantra with detailed instructions for its installation. The visit lasts two hours, after which Babaji departs. The narrator reflects on the profound impact of the encounter and later receives mental messages from Babaji, including a vision of Babaji living incognito in Benares until late 2019.

beats.title: The Awakener — The Identity of Babaji
beats.summary: This chapter explores the mystery of Babaji's identity, discussing various theories (Gorakhnath, Tamil Siddha, Svatmarama) and emphasizing that Babaji's true origin remains unknown. It highlights the importance of respecting the Guru's secrecy and the dangers of summoning him for trivial reasons.

beats.title: The Awakener — Siddhis, Small Miracles and Magic Tricks
beats.summary: The chapter explores the siddhis (supernatural powers) attained through yogic mastery, focusing on control over the five elements, invisibility, materialization, and revival of insects. It distinguishes genuine yogic achievements from mere magic tricks, emphasizing the importance of inner light and ethical motivation. The narrative includes personal experiments with ancient tantric texts and a pivotal realization to avoid spiritual showmanship.

beats.title: The Awakener — Shivalingam
beats.summary: The chapter describes the discovery of a natural Shivalingam on Govardhan Hill, its installation at the cremation site of a Maharaja, and the subsequent establishment of a temple. It includes the events leading to the find, the ritual installation, and the significance of the Shivalingam in the context of Braj.

beats.title: The Awakener — Nepal. Ritual (part 1)
beats.summary: Shailendra Sharma recounts his journey to Nepal to learn proper Shiva puja from an old priest at Pashupatinath, the discovery of a natural Shivalingam at a cremation ground in Braj, and the subsequent establishment of a Shiva temple there. The chapter weaves together themes of ritual, sacred geography, and the interplay of Shiva and Shakti energies.

beats.title: The Awakener — Nepal. Ritual (part 2)
beats.summary: The chapter describes the discovery of Shakti Pitha signs at a smashana (cremation ground) in Braj, including stone fragments resembling female reproductive organs, a bronze statuette of a four-armed goddess, and other miraculous events. It narrates the arrival of the Shri Yantra, the appearance of Babaji and Mahadeva, and the installation of the Shivalingam. Three Natha yogins visit, revealing that the installation awakened the Avadhuta (Dattatreya) and that the Nathas are immortals. The chapter also explains yantras and their purposes, and describes the ongoing manifestation of yantras at the site.

beats.title: The Awakener — Nepal. Ritual (part 3)
beats.summary: The narrator recounts the acquisition of yantras for the Mahavidyas, the meeting with the immortal Matsyendranatha at Mahurgad, the installation of the dhuni (yogic fire) at the smashana, and the subsequent nightly visitation of spirits. The chapter also includes legends of Matsyendranatha's origin and the nature of the Mahavidyas.

beats.title: The Awakener — Nepal. Ritual (part 4)
beats.summary: The chapter describes the narrator's life at a cremation ground (smashana) in Govardhan, focusing on the significance of kundalas (earrings) in the Natha tradition, the symbolism of the Ouroboros bracelet, the nature of Aghora, and the role of the smashana in advanced yogic practice. It includes anecdotes about obtaining rhinoceros horn, piercing earlobes, and encounters with supernatural beings.

beats.title: The Awakener — Nepal. Ritual (part 5)
beats.summary: The chapter describes the installation of a Shivalingam at a smashana (cremation ground) in Govardhan, Nepal, and the subsequent arrival of Dattatreya, the Avadhuta, who includes the temple in his nightly route. It explores the significance of Dattatreya as the Guru of Yoga, the relationship between Shiva and Shakti, the role of Babaji's lineage in promoting women's spiritual equality, and the transition to a new yuga of Yoga. The narrative also touches on the Nathas' tradition, the summoning of the Avadhuta, and the hidden secrets of India's alchemical gold and immortals.
